Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test sends a bundle to an STCPCL induct and confirms the initial 32-bit integer of the bundle is read as the bundle length and is used to read the correct number of bytes from the TCP stream.\

Title: Unidirectional Link
Requirement: Each STCPCL link shall be unidirectional.
Rationale: STCPCL is a simplified version of TCPCL. It is only implemented to support legacy systems.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when an STCP link has been shown as unidirectional.\

Title: Bundle Transmission
Requirement: Bundles shall be transmitted over the connected TCP socket.
Rationale: Simple Transmission Control Protocol (STCP) was implemented to ensure compatibility with legacy implementation.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when bundles are transmitted over the TCP connection.\

Title: Bundle Transmission Length
Requirement: Bundles transmitted on the connection shall be preceded by a 32-bit unsigned integer in network byte order indicating the length of the bundle.
Rationale: The 32-bit length frames the bundle within a TCP stream.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test receives a bundle from an STCPCL outduct and confirms the value of the 32-bit integer preceding the bundle matches the length of the bundle.\

Title: STCPCL Keep-alive
Requirement: Keep-alive packets shall be indicated by a 32-bit unsigned integer with all bits set to zero.
Rationale: Keep-alive packets have been implemented to ensure compatibility with legacy implementation STCPCL.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test monitors an STCPCL outduct and confirms the TCP session is maintained by periodic reception of data or a 32-bit unsigned integer with all bits set to zero.\

Title: STCPCL Keep-alive Interval
Requirement: Keep-alive packets shall be transmitted at a user-specified interval when no data is transmitted.
Rationale: Keep-alive packets have been implemented to ensure compatibility with legacy implementation STCPCL.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test monitors an STCPCL outduct and confirms that keep-alives are transmitted at the user- specified interval.\

Title: Maximum Bundle Length
Requirement: Accepted bundles shall be less than or equal to a user- specified maximum length.
Rationale: Very large bundles could consume a large amount of memory.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test sends an oversized bundle to an STCPCL induct and confirms the connection is terminated.\

Title: BP Over a Datagram CL
Requirement: A UDP datagram shall contain one bundle.
Rationale: To utilize DTN protocols across the Internet, encapsulating them into standard protocols is necessary. UDP is a unidirectional protocol with no congestion control. One bundle per datagram simplifies the convergence layer.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test receives a datagram from a UDPCL outduct and confirms the datagram contains exactly one bundle.\

Title: Rate Limit
Requirement: UDP outduct transmission rates shall be limited by a user-specific rate limit.
Rationale: UDP packets will be dropped if they exceed the expected link rate.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test receives data from a UDPCL outduct and confirms the received data rate does not exceed the configured rate.\

Title: Port Configuration Listening
Requirement: UDP induct shall be bound to a user-provided port.
Rationale: UDP connections are to be established via a known port.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test successfully connects to a UDPCL induct utilizing the expected port.\

Title: Port Configuration Transmission
Requirement: UDP outduct shall connect to a user-provided port.
Rationale: UDP connections are to be established via a known port.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test receives a connection from a UDPCL outduct utilizing the expected port.\

Title: Session ID
Requirement: A session ID shall consist of the sender's engine ID and a session number randomly generated by the sender.
Rationale: This is the definition of the session ID.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that a series of session IDs consists of the sender's engine ID and a session number randomly generated by the sender.\

Title: Session ID Unique
Requirement: A session ID shall uniquely identify every session initiated by an LTP engine.
Rationale: Each session needs a unique ID to distinguish it from other sessions.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that a series of bundles transmitted from an LTP outduct contains unique session IDs.\

Title: Incrementing Checkpoint Serial Numbers
Requirement: Any subsequent checkpoints issued by the sender shall have the serial number value found by incrementing the prior checkpoint serial number by 1.
Rationale: The checkpoint serial number uniquely identifies the checkpoint among all checkpoints issued by the block sender in a session.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that a series of checkpoint serial numbers increment by one for each subsequent checkpoint.\

Title: Retransmitted Checkpoints
Requirement: When a checkpoint segment is retransmitted, its serial number shall be the same as initially transmitted.
Rationale: Retransmitted serial numbers should not be changed from the original serial number since they are related to the same checkpoint.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the serial number for a retransmitted checkpoint is the same as the serial number of the original checkpoint.\

Title: Report Serial Number
Requirement: When a Report Segment (RS) is received in response to a checkpoint, the LTP sender shall transmit a Report Acknowledgement (RA) segment that includes the same report serial number as the received RS.
Rationale: This is required according to section 6.13 of RFC 5326. A non-zero value identifies it as a response to the reception.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the reported serial number of a received RA matches the serial number of the RS that caused the acknowledgment to be queued for transmission.\

Title: Report Segment
Requirement: Any subsequent Report Segment issued by the receiver shall have the serial number value found by incrementing the last report serial number by 1.
Rationale: It must be implemented per RFC 5326 section 3.2.2 and helps ensure interoperability with other DTNs.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that a series of Report Segments have a report serial number that increments by 1.\

Title: Retransmitted Report Segment
Requirement: When a Report Segment is retransmitted, its serial number shall be the same as initially transmitted.
Rationale: It must be implemented per RFC 5326 section 3.2.2 and helps ensure interoperability with other DTNs.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that a retransmitted Report Segment contains its original serial number.\

Title: Reception Claims Length Limits
Requirement: The sum of an LTP Reception Claim's length and offset shall not exceed the difference between the upper and lower bounds of the report segment.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 3.2.2. The upper and lower bounds define report segment location and the reception claim is a portion of the segment.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the sum of an LTP Reception Claim's length and offset is between the upper and lower bounds of the report segment.\

Title: Reception Claims Offset
Requirement: An LTP Reception Claim's offset shall be greater than the sum of the offset and the length of the prior claim.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 3.2.2. The LTP Reception Claim should not overlap with a prior claim.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that an LTP Reception Claim's offset is greater than the offset's sum and the prior claim's length.\

Title: Reception Claims Upper Bound
Requirement: An LTP Reception Claim's upper bound shall be greater than or equal to the offset, length, and lower bound sum.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 3.2.2. It defines the LTP Reception Claim's location in a report segment.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that an LTP Reception Claim's upper bound is greater than or equal to the sum of the offset, length, and lower bound.\

Title: Session Management Segments
Requirement: An LTP Session Management Segment shall consist of a cancel segment or cancel acknowledgment.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 3.2.4. Section 3.2.4 defines the contents of a Session Management Segment.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that an LTP Session Management Segment contains either a cancel segment or cancel acknowledgment.\

Title: End of Block
Requirement: The last data segment in a block shall be marked as the end of block (EOB).
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 4.1. This determines where the end of the block is located.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the last data segment in a block is marked as the EOB.\

Title: Data Segment
Requirement: An LTP data segment shall only contain either red-part data, green-part data, or both.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 4.1. Red and green data segments pertain to the reliability applied to the data segment. Data can be sent reliably, unreliably or a combination of both.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that an LTP data segment contains either a red-part, a green- part, or both.\

Title: End of Red-Part
Requirement: The Last Data Segment for Red-part data shall be marked as the end of red-part (EORP) segment.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 4.1. This is needed to determine where the end of red-part data occurs.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the last data segment for a red- part data is marked as the EORP segment.\

Title: Maximum Transmission Unit
Requirement: Data shall be subdivided into data segments within a user- specified maximum transmission unit size.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 4.1. Data segments must fit into the framing size of the underlying network layers. This will be specific to the user's particular network.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that data segments are within the specified maximum transmission unit size.\

Title: Requirements from the Operating Environment
Requirement: LTP shall be run directly over a data-link layer protocol.
Rationale: LTP is meant to provide additional reliability on top of an existing data link layer. HDTN currently supports UDP as the data-link protocol.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows a successful LTP communication session over UDP as a data-link layer protocol.\

Title: LTP Link Status
Requirement: The LTP Engine shall detect the status of an LTP destination.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 5. LTP Engine must detect whether the link was brought up or shut down.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the LTP Engine detects if the link to the destination is active.\

Title: One-way Light Time
Requirement: The LTP Engine shall read the current distance from the configuration file.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 5. This is used to calculate timeout intervals.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the one-way light time parameter is read correctly from the configuration file.\

Title: Local Data-link Layer Protocols
Requirement: The content of each local data-link layer protocol frame shall contain an integer number of LTP segments.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 5. Fractions of LTP segments would lack the needed context and could become invalid if received out of order.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the content of each local data- link layer protocol frame is an integral number of LTP segments.\

Title: Invalid Segments
Requirement: The LTP Engine shall discard invalid segments.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 6. LTP segments that do not conform to the specification are discarded.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the LTP Engine discards invalid segments.\

Title: UNREACH Reason Code
Requirement: The LTP Engine shall send a Cancel by block Receiver (CR) with reason-code UNREACH if the invalid data segment contains red-part data.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 6. Red- part data must be transmitted reliably and the LTP engine must signal if an error has occurred.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the LTP Engine sends a CR with reason-code UNREACH when an invalid data segment contains red-part data.\

Title: Retransmit Checkpoint
Requirement: The expiration of a countdown timer associated with a Checkpoint (CP) segment shall invoke the Cancel Session procedure for the session associated with this segment.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 6.7. As countdowns expire, they must end the corresponding session via the cancel session procedure and retransmission.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the expiration of a countdown timer associated with a CP segment triggers the Cancel Session procedure.\

Title: Retransmit RS
Requirement: When the number of times any affected RS segment has been queued for transmission exceeds the report retransmission limit established for the local LTP engine, the "Cancel Session" procedure shall be invoked.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 6.8. The retransmission limit prevents excessive attempts to retransmit data.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the "cancel session" procedure is invoked when the retransmission attempts of RS segment(s) exceed the report retransmission limit.\

Title: Signify Red-Part Reception
Requirement: Upon the arrival of a CP segment, when the EORP for this session has been received, and all data in the red-part of the block being transmitted in this session have been received, the LTP engine shall send a red-part reception notice to the specified client service.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 6.9. The LTP engine needs to notify the sender when all red- part has been received.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the LTP engine sends a red- part reception notice to the specified client service upon the arrival of a CP segment at the end-of-red, and all red-part data in the block being transmitted in this session has been received.\

Title: Send Reception Report
Requirement: When the number of reception problems detected for this session exceeds a limit established for the local LTP engine, the "Cancel Session" procedure shall be invoked.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 6.11. Resources must be freed if the canceling session exceeds the reception problems limit.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the cancel session procedure is invoked when the number of reception problems detected for this session exceeds the limit established for the local LTP engine.\

Title: Signify Transmission Completion Notice
Requirement: A transmission-session completion notice shall be sent to the local client service associated with the session when these conditions have been met: 1. Data in the block is known to have been transmitted. 2. The entire red-part of the block is known to have been successfully received.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 6.12. Need to notify sender when entire transmission has been received, and all data is accounted for.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that a transmission-session completion notice is sent to the local client service associated with the session when these conditions have been met: 1. Data in the block is known to have been transmitted. 2. The entire red-part of the block is known to have been successfully received.\

Title: Reason Code RLEXC
Requirement: When the number of transmission problems for this session exceeds a limit established for the local LTP engine, a CS with reason- code Retransmission limit exceeded (RLEXC) shall be appended to the transmission queue specified in the transmission request that started this session, and a transmission-session cancellation notice is sent back to the client service that requested the transmission.
Rationale: There should only be a finite number of retransmission attempts. See section RFC 5326 section 7.5 for the transmission session cancellation notice.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the number of transmission problems for a session exceeds a limit established for the local LTP engine. Also, a CS with reason code RLEXC shall be appended to the transmission queue specified in the transmission request that started the session, and a transmission-session cancellation notice is sent back to the client service that requested the transmission.\

Title: Stop RS Timer
Requirement: The countdown timer associated with the original RS segment (identified by the report serial number of the Report-Acknowledgment (RA) segment) shall be deleted upon an RA reception.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 6.14. Resources should be freed by ending countdown timers associated with RS segments.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the bundle agent stops sending Report Segments (RS) for an LTP data segment once a Report Acknowledge (RA) segment is received.\

Title: CS Start Cancel Timer
Requirement: Upon the arrival of a link state cue indicating the de- queuing (for transmission) of a CS segment, a countdown timer for the expected arrival time of the Cancel Acknowledgment to block Sender (CAS) segment shall be started.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 6.15. Timers are needed to diagnose connection conditions.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test sends an LTP CAS segment after receiving two LTP CS segments and it does not receive an additional LTP CS segment.\

Title: CR Start Cancel Timer
Requirement: Upon the arrival of a link state cue indicating the de- queuing (for transmission) of a CR segment, a countdown timer for the expected arrival time of the Cancel- Acknowledgment segment to block Receiver (CAR) segment shall be started.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 6.15. Timers are needed to diagnose connection conditions.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test sends an LTP CAR segment after receiving two LTP CR segments and does not receive any additional LTP CR segments.\

Title: CS Acknowledge Cancellation
Requirement: When a CS segment has a transmission queue-set bound for the sender, a CAS segment shall be appended to the queue of internal operations traffic bound for the sender.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 6.17. The LTP engine needs to acknowledge when a cancel session segment is received.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test sends an LTP CS segment and receives an LTP CAS segment in response.\

Title: CR Acknowledge Cancellation
Requirement: When a CR segment has a transmission queue-set bound for the sender, a CAR (cancel acknowledgment to block receiver) segment shall be appended to the queue of internal operations traffic bound for the receiver.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 6.17. The LTP engine needs to acknowledge when a cancel session segment is received.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test sends an LTP CR segment and receives an LTP CAR segment in response.\

Title: CAS Stop Cancel Timer
Requirement: Upon reception of a CAS segment, the timer associated with the CS segment shall be deleted.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 6.18. Resources must be freed by ending countdown timers associated with CS segments.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test sends an LTP CAS segment after receiving two LTP CS segments and no additional LTP CS segments are received.\

Title: CAR Stop Cancel Timer
Requirement: Upon reception of a CAR segment, the timer associated with the CR segment shall be deleted.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 6.18. Resources must be freed by ending countdown timers associated with CR segments.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test sends an LTP CAR segment after receiving two LTP CR segments and no additional LTP CR segments are received.\

Title: Cancel Session
Requirement: When a session is canceled, the LTP engine shall delete all queued segments from outbound traffic queues.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 6.19. Resources associated with a canceled session must be freed.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that a cancel acknowledgement for the current session is received and no additional LTP segments are provided with the cancelled session number after a cancel segment is issued.\

Title: Countdown Timers
Requirement: When a session is canceled, the LTP engine shall delete all countdown timers currently associated with the session.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 6.19. Resources associated with a canceled session must be freed.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that a cancel acknowledgement for the current session is received and no additional LTP segments are provided with the cancelled session number after a cancel segment is issued.\

Title: Close Session
Requirement: The remaining countdown timers associated with a closed session shall be deleted.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 6.20. Resources associated with a closed session must be freed.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test sends an LTP RS segment with a session number from a completed session, and confirms that no LTP RA segment is received.\

Title: Handling Miscolored Segments
Requirement: When miscolored data blocks are received, the LTP engine shall discard them.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 6.21. Miscolored data blocks need to be removed from the system. This procedure is triggered by the arrival of either (a) a red-part data segment whose block offset begins at an offset higher than the block offset of any green- part data segment previously received for the same session or (b) a green-part data segment whose block offset is lower than the block offset of any red-part data segment previously received for the same session. The arrival of a segment matching either of the above checks violates the protocol requirement of having all red-part data as the block prefix and all green-part data as the block suffix.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that miscolored data blocks are discarded.\

Title: Cancel Session for Miscolored Data
Requirement: The local LTP engine shall cancel the session when miscolored data blocks are received.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 6.21. The LTP engine should prevent further miscolored blocks from entering the system.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the LTP session is canceled when miscolored data has been received.\

Title: UDP Port Number for LTP
Requirement: The UDP port number shall be user-configurable.
Rationale: This needs to be implemented per RFC 5326 section 10.1.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the LTP port is configured based on the configuration file.\

Title: BPGen Bundle Size
Requirement: The BPGen application shall generate bundles with the size specified.
Rationale: The bundle size is configurable in support of benchmark testing.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows the transmitted bundle payload size matches the user- specified size.\

Title: BPGen Aggregate Custody Signals
Requirement: The BPGen application shall allow aggregate custody signal custody transfer usage.
Rationale: Aggregate custody signals provide bundle layer reliability when using the Bundle Protocol version 6.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows the BPGen application is configured to use aggregate custody signals when a Bundle Protocol Version 6 is configured, and bundles are transmitted successfully.\

Title: BPGen Bidirectional Communication
Requirement: The BPGen application shall support bidirectional communication when receiving custody signals.
Rationale: Bidirectional communication is needed for acknowledgments when using the Bundle Protocol version 6.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows the BPGen application is configured to use custody signals when a Bundle Protocol Version 6 is configured, and bundles are transmitted successfully.\

Title: BPGen Bundle Priority
Requirement: The BPGen application shall have the option to specify the bundle priority from the predefined range for BP version 6.
Rationale: Bundle priority is a part of RFC 5050 and QoS support.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the bundle priority field of a transmitted bundle matches the specified priority.\

Title: BPGen Statistics
Requirement: The BPGen application shall keep statistics of bundle count and order.
Rationale: Statistics are needed for debugging purposes.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that bundle count and bundle order statistics are generated correctly.\

Title: BPGen Rate
Requirement: The BPGen application shall transfer bundles at a specified rate.
Rationale: Convergence layers such as LTP and UDP must have a rate set, or bundles will be dropped.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the bundles per second generated (approximately/are within 10%) match the specified rate.\

Title: BPGen Duration
Requirement: The BPGen application shall send bundles for a specified duration.
Rationale: The user should be able to set a time for the application to stop sending data.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the application stops sending bundles after the specified duration.\

Title: BPGen Destination
Requirement: The BPGen application shall send bundle data to a specified EID.
Rationale: The application needs a destination Endpoint ID to send bundles to.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the transmitted bundles' bundle destination EID field matches the specified EID.\

Title: BPGen Source
Requirement: The BPGen application shall send bundle data with a specified source EID.
Rationale: The application needs to specify a source Endpoint ID.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the transmitted bundles' bundle source EID field matches the specified EID.\

Title: BPGen Timeout
Requirement: The BPGen application shall allow the user to specify a maximum timeout.
Rationale: A timeout should be specified to determine if bundles are being received. This works for convergence layers that support acknowledgments.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test using a TCP- based outduct shows an error message is printed when BPGen is sending bundles faster than the receiver is reading them for the specified number of seconds.\

Title: BPGen Bundle Lifetime
Requirement: The BPGen application shall allow the user to set a maximum bundle lifetime.
Rationale: A bundle lifetime is needed to determine how long bundles should be kept in storage.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows the transmitted bundles' bundle time to live field matches the specified bundle lifetime.\

Title: BPGen Convergence-layer adapter (CLA) Rate
Requirement: The BPGen application shall allow the user to specify a CLA rate.
Rationale: The application will configure with the provided value if the convergence layer supports the rate configuration.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows that bundles are transmitted according to the specified rate. This only pertains to UDP and LTP.\

Title: BPGen LTP Convergence Layer
Requirement: The BPGen application shall support the LTP convergence layer.
Rationale: The convergence layer requirements are implemented as part of the HDTN development. This application can be configured to use the convergence layer.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows the BPGen application is configured to use an LTP convergence layer and bundles are transmitted successfully.\

Title: BPGen TCP Convergence Layer
Requirement: The BPGen application shall support the TCP version 4 convergence layer.
Rationale: The convergence layer requirements are implemented as part of the HDTN development . This application can be configured to use the convergence layer.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows the BPGen application is configured to use a TCP v4 convergence layer, and bundles are received successfully.\

Title: BPGen UDP Convergence Layer
Requirement: The BPGen application shall support the UDP convergence layer.
Rationale: The convergence layer requirements are implemented as part of the HDTN development. This application can be configured to use the convergence layer.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows the BPGen application is configured to use a UDP convergence layer and bundles are received successfully.\

Title: BPing Creation Timestamp
Requirement: The BPing application shall create a custom payload bundle with the creation timestamp.
Rationale: The timestamp is used to calculate bundle life elapsed time.

Title: BPing Destination Endpoint ID
Requirement: The BPing application shall send the custom payload bundle to a specified node and service ID.
Rationale: The node will be the end recipient of the bundle. The service ID should be an ID that correlates to an "ECHO" service, which will generate a response.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that bundles are sent to and received by the specified node and service ID.\

Title: BPing Elapsed Time Calculation
Requirement: The BPing application shall calculate the time elapsed from received payload bundles with specified service ID.
Rationale: This calculation is used to know how long it took to receive a response.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows the correct time elapsed is calculated to receive a bundle.\

Title: BPing Elapsed Time Display
Requirement: The BPing application shall display the calculated time elapsed to the user.
Rationale: This provides the user with information about the network.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the correct time elapsed to receive a bundle is printed to standard out.\

Title: BPing Send Duration

Rationale: The application can be configured to send multiple bundles to calculate the responses' average duration.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the application stops sending bundles after the specified duration.\

Title: BPing Response Received
Requirement: The BPing application shall send a new custom payload bundle after receiving a response.
Rationale: The application will continuously generate a new bundle after receiving a response unless a specific number is provided or the application is stopped.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that a new bundle is sent after receiving a response.\

Title: BPReceiveStream Bundle Protocol Version 6
Requirement: The BPReceiveStream application shall support Bundle Protocol version 6.
Rationale: The convergence layer requirements are implemented as part of the HDTN development. This application can be configured to use the convergence layer.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows the BPReceiveStream application is configured to use a Bundle Protocol v6 convergence layer and bundles are received successfully.\

Title: BPReceiveStream Bundle Protocol Version 7
Requirement: The BPReceiveStream application shall support Bundle Protocol version 7.
Rationale: The convergence layer requirements are implemented as part of the HDTN development. This application can be configured to use the convergence layer.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows the BPReceiveStream application is configured to use a Bundle Protocol v7 convergence layer and bundles are received successfully.\

Title: BPReceiveStream BPSec Support
Requirement: The BPReceiveStream application shall support BPSec (RFC9172/RFC9173).
Rationale: The convergence layer requirements are implemented as part of the HDTN development. This application can be configured to use the convergence layer.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows the BPReceiveStream application is configured to use BpSec and bundles are received successfully.\

Title: BPReceiveStream Destination
Requirement: The BPReceiveStream application shall receive bundle data with a specified EID.
Rationale: The application needs a destination Endpoint ID to receive bundles.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the received bundles' bundle destination EID field matches the specified EID.\

Title: BPReceiveStream TCP Convergence Layer
Requirement: The BPReceiveStream application shall support the TCP version 4 convergence layer.
Rationale: The convergence layer requirements are implemented as part of the HDTN development. This application can be configured to use the convergence layer.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows the BPReceiveStream application is configured to use a TCP v4 convergence layer and bundles are received successfully.\

Title: BPReceiveStream UDP Convergence Layer
Requirement: The BPReceiveStream application shall support the UDP convergence layer.
Rationale: The convergence layer requirements are implemented as part of the HDTN development. This application can be configured to use the convergence layer.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows the BPReceiveStream application is configured to use a UDP convergence layer and bundles are received successfully.\

Title: BPReceiveStream STCP Convergence Layer
Requirement: The BPReceiveStream application shall support the STCP convergence layer.
Rationale: The convergence layer requirements are implemented as part of the HDTN development. This application can be configured to use the convergence layer.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows the BPReceiveStream application is configured to use an STCP convergence layer and bundles are received successfully.\

Title: BPReceiveStream LTP Convergence Layer
Requirement: The BPReceiveStream application shall support the LTP convergence layer.
Rationale: The convergence layer requirements are implemented as part of the HDTN development. This application can be configured to use the convergence layer.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows the BPReceiveStream application is configured to use an LTP convergence layer and bundles are transmitted successfully.\

Title: Static Routing Configuration
Requirement: The HDTN agent shall provide the ability to configure static routes.
Rationale: Static routes are useful for simple network topologies and as a fallback routing method.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows a static route can be configured and a bundle is routed through the specified route.\

Title: Static Routing Next Hop
Requirement: Static routes shall specify the next hop for a given destination.
Rationale: Static routing needs to define where a bundle is sent based on its destination.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that a bundle sent to a given destination is sent to the configured next hop.\

Title: Static Routing Default
Requirement: A default static route shall apply to all bundles not covered by another static route.
Rationale: A default static route simplifies network configuration by reducing the number of individual routes needed.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test sends a bundle to a destination not covered by an existing static route and it is sent to the default next hop.\

Title: Schedule Aware Bundle Routing
Requirement: HDTN shall be configurable to use the Schedule-Aware Bundle Routing (SABR) protocol as specified in CCSDS 734.3-B-1.
Rationale: SABR provides routing based on a known schedule of contacts in a DTN.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows a bundle being routed according to a contact plan when SABR is enabled.\

Title: Linux OS Support
Requirement: The HDTN CSCI shall run on the Linux operating system.
Rationale: The Linux operating system is used on many NASA systems, including the ISS.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows the HDTN software operating on a Linux operating system.\

Title: C++ 14
Requirement: The HDTN CSCI shall be built using the C++ 14 compiler.
Rationale: C++ 14 provides the needed features to develop the HDTN software.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a review of the HDTN software development environment shows C++ 14 compiler is being used.\

Title: Make Build System
Requirement: The HDTN CSCI shall be built using the Make build system.
Rationale: Make is a common build automation tool for the Linux operating system.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows the HDTN CSCI can be built using the Make build system.\

Title: CMake Build System
Requirement: The HDTN CSCI shall provide a CMake build system.
Rationale: CMake is a common build automation tool.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows the HDTN CSCI can be built using the CMake build system.\

Title: OpenSSL FIPS 140-3
Requirement: The HDTN CSCI shall require the OpenSSL FIPS 140-3 validated cryptographic module.
Rationale: NPR 2810.1A requires that NASA systems use FIPS-validated cryptography.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows that the HDTN CSCI fails to build if OpenSSL with FIPS is not installed.\

Title: Configuration Files
Requirement: The HDTN CSCI shall read its configuration from a user- supplied configuration file.
Rationale: Configuration files allow flexibility in configuring the HDTN CSCI.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows the HDTN CSCI successfully reading configuration options from a configuration file.\

Title: API Bindings
Requirement: The HDTN API shall provide C++ language bindings.
Rationale: C++ is the primary development language for HDTN and is the only supported language for the API.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when an inspection of the source code reveals a C++ API header.\

Title: API Bundle Data
Requirement: The HDTN API shall receive bundle data to be transmitted.
Rationale: The HDTN API provides an interface for external applications to send bundles.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows an application can provide a buffer to be sent in a bundle to the HDTN API.\

Title: API Bundle Metadata
Requirement: The HDTN API shall receive bundle metadata to be transmitted.
Rationale: Bundle metadata provides information used for routing and network management.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows an application can provide bundle metadata to the HDTN API.\

Title: API Bundle Data Reception
Requirement: The HDTN API shall deliver bundle data to the receiving application.
Rationale: The HDTN API provides an interface for external applications to receive bundles.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows an application can receive a buffer with bundle payload data from the HDTN API.\

Title: API Bundle Metadata Reception
Requirement: The HDTN API shall deliver bundle metadata to the receiving application.
Rationale: Bundle metadata provides information used for routing and network management.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows an application can receive bundle metadata from the HDTN API.\

Title: API Error Reporting
Requirement: The HDTN API shall report errors to the application.
Rationale: Error reporting is important for troubleshooting issues with the application and network.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the API reports an error message to the application when an error is encountered.\

Title: API Connection Status
Requirement: The HDTN API shall report the connection status to the application.
Rationale: The connection status allows the application to respond to network availability.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test shows that the API reports a connected status when connected to the HDTN agent and a disconnected status when it is not.\

Title: API Connection Timeout
Requirement: The HDTN API shall report an error when a connection to the HDTN CSCI cannot be established within a timeout period.
Rationale: A timeout is needed to avoid an indefinite wait when the HDTN CSCI is unavailable.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a test attempts to connect to a non-existent HDTN CSCI and an error is returned after a timeout.\

Title: GUI Display Storage Metrics
Requirement: The HDTN GUI shall display the metrics described in Table E-1 Storage Metrics.
Rationale: These metrics are specified as the minimum required for network operators.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows the GUI displaying the required Storage Metrics.\

Title: GUI Display LTP Metrics
Requirement: The HDTN GUI shall display the metrics described in Table E-2 LTP Metrics.
Rationale: These metrics are specified as the minimum required for network operators.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows the GUI displaying the required LTP Metrics.\

Title: GUI Display STCP Metrics
Requirement: The HDTN GUI shall display the metrics described in Table E-3 STCP Metrics.
Rationale: These metrics are specified as the minimum required for network operators.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows the GUI displaying the required STCP Metrics.\

Title: GUI Display TCP Metrics
Requirement: The HDTN GUI shall display the metrics described in Table E-4 TCP Metrics.
Rationale: These metrics are specified as the minimum required for network operators.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows the GUI displaying the required TCP Metrics.\

Title: GUI Display UDP Metrics
Requirement: The HDTN GUI shall display the metrics described in Table E-5 UDP Metrics.
Rationale: These metrics are specified as the minimum required for network operators.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows the GUI displaying the required UDP Metrics.\

Title: GUI Update Rate
Requirement: The HDTN GUI shall update at a user-configurable rate.
Rationale: The update rate should be configurable to adjust to operator needs and network conditions.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows the GUI update rate can be configured by the user.\

Title: GUI Configuration
Requirement: The HDTN GUI shall allow the user to specify the configuration for the HDTN agent.
Rationale: The GUI should be able to start and stop the HDTN agent.
Verification Statement: This requirement is verified when a demonstration shows the user can configure and start the HDTN agent from the GUI.\

Title: System View Display
Requirement: The user interface shall display a system view showing the HDTN components and how data flows through the system.
Rationale: The system view provides a way to quickly understand the current state of HDTN and its main components.
Verification Statement: This requirement is considered verified when a demonstration is performed where the GUI displays a system view with HDTN components and shows how data flows through the system.\

Title: System View Display Preferences
Requirement: The user interface shall provide display preference options for the system view.
Rationale: This allows the system view to adapt to different screen sizes and user preferences, including font size and color theme.
Verification Statement: This requirement is considered verified when a demonstration is performed where the GUI correctly displays on various screen sizes.\

Title: Ping Via GUI
Requirement: The user interface shall support sending a ping command.
Rationale: This lets the user quickly determine whether a connection exists to the given node.
Verification Statement: This requirement is considered verified when a demonstration is performed where the GUI can send a ping to a requested node.\

Title: GUI Outduct Display
Requirement: The system view shall display metrics when the cursor is over the outduct.
Rationale: This provides insight into the behavior of the convergence layers.
Verification Statement: This requirement is considered verified when a demonstration is performed where the GUI displays outduct metrics when the cursor is over the outduct.\

Title: GUI Induct Display
Requirement: The system view shall display metrics when the cursor is over the induct.
Rationale: This provides insight into the behavior of the convergence layers.
Verification Statement: This requirement is considered verified when a demonstration is performed where the GUI displays induct metrics when the cursor is over the induct.\

Title: GUI Storage Display
Requirement: The system view shall display storage component metrics when the cursor is over the storage module.
Rationale: This allows storage usage and capacity to be monitored.
Verification Statement: This requirement is considered verified when a demonstration is performed where the GUI displays storage metrics when the cursor is over the storage module.}
